subsection.name: Description
subsection.div:
Checks the placement of right curly braces ('}') for code blocks. The right curly brace must be alone on a line. Empty blocks must follow K&R style or use {} immediately, except in multi-block statements (if/else, try/catch/finally) where {} is not allowed.
Follows Google Java Style Guide.

tr.td: Allow RCURLY on the same line as the start of a multiblock statement.

subset of tokens
LITERAL_TRY , LITERAL_CATCH , LITERAL_FINALLY , LITERAL_IF , LITERAL_ELSE , CLASS_DEF , METHOD_DEF , CTOR_DEF , LITERAL_FOR , LITERAL_WHILE , LITERAL_DO , STATIC_INIT , INSTANCE_INIT , ANNOTATION_DEF , ENUM_DEF , INTERFACE_DEF , RECORD_DEF , COMPACT_CTOR_DEF , LITERAL_SWITCH , LITERAL_DEFAULT , LITERAL_CASE .

subsection.name: Examples
subsection.p: To configure the check for specific tokens:

pre.code: <module name="Checker"> <module name="TreeWalker"> <module name="RightCurlyAloneOrEmpty"> <property name="tokens" value="CLASS_DEF, METHOD_DEF"/> </module> </module> </module>

subsection.p: To configure the allow multi statements (if/else, try/catch/finally on same line as '}'):

subsection.name: Violation Messages
subsection.p: All messages can be customized if the default message doesn't suit you. Please see the documentation to learn how to.

subsection.name: Fully Qualified Name
subsection.p: Use this fully qualified class name in configuration when an exact class reference is required.
